title: Session Credits P3.1 - Package Issuance Test Strategy

children.title: Validate package-based session credit issuance triggers only when payment, activation, and Booking gate all pass
Ensure purchase snapshot rules remain stable for existing purchases after package edits
Verify recurring, trial, recharge, and late-activation issuance paths do not over-issue or skip credits incorrectly
Confirm purchase and invoice status redefinition stays consistent across details, filters, and actions
Verify source linkage, notification, and history behavior for package-issued credits
In Scope
Package pricing and session credit configuration
Pricing model, free trial, rule count, quantity, expiration, session type eligibility
Published package edit rules and publish blocking for invalid session types
Credit issuance engine
One-time, recurring, trial, recharge recovery, late activation, archived client
Snapshot usage, expiration anchor, cycle-level issuance marker
Purchase and invoice lifecycle
Active, Free Trial, Overdue, Paused, Expires Soon, Expired, Cancels Soon, Cancelled
Pending, Paid, Overdue, Failed, Expired, Refunded invoice states
Client and coach surfaces
Purchase Details, Balance History, Client Overview, Sessions Credits tab
Permission-sensitive source links and issuance notifications
Feature flag and migration behavior
Booking OFF, Payment and Packages OFF, pre-P3.1 package and purchase migration
Out of Scope
Issuing credits during free trial
Automatic deletion of credits when Booking is disabled
Client-facing package-page session credit display
Bulk credit management and coach-level reporting
Mobile Package future integrations beyond current notification navigation
Unit Test
Issuance gate, expiration anchor, quantity/rule validations, status transition helpers
Integration Testing
Payment success and recharge events, activation event, feature flag checks, persistence of issuance markers
System Testing
End-to-end package purchase to credit balance and history update across one-time and recurring models
UI/UX Testing
Pricing modal, purchase details, purchase filters, client overview, source-link behavior
Regression Testing
Existing payment flows, trial flows, pause/cancel/reactivate actions, Balance History, Sessions tab
Sanity Test
Critical paid purchase issue path, one recurring renewal, one non-clickable permission case
Smoke Test
Package creation, paid purchase, credit issuance visibility, no duplicate issuance
Functional Testing
Positive issuance flows for one-time, trial, recurring, recharge, and late activation
Negative blocking flows for invalid session types, failed cycles, and feature-flag suppression
Testing Types
UI testing for package pricing and purchase details
API and webhook testing for payment and activation ordering
Data integrity testing for snapshot persistence, issuance markers, and history linkage
Notification and permission testing for coach-facing issuance updates
Test Design Techniques
Decision Table Testing for payment + activation + Booking gate
State Transition Testing for purchase and invoice lifecycles
Boundary Value Analysis for quantity, rule count, billing cycle, and expiration inputs
Equivalence Partitioning for pricing model, trial mode, activation path, and feature flags
Error Guessing for webhook retries, stale tabs, duplicate activation, and migration gaps
Automation Approaches
API and integration automation for issuance gate, recharge recovery, and marker idempotency
UI regression automation for package pricing, purchase details, and client balance visibility
Targeted event-driven tests for payment webhook replay and late-activation catch-up
Workspace configurations
Booking ON/OFF, Payment and Packages ON/OFF, Stripe connected vs not connected
Package variants
One-time without trial, one-time with trial, recurring without trial, recurring with trial
1-rule and multi-rule packages with distinct session types
Boundary values
Rule quantity 0, 1, 100, 101
Rule count 0, 1, 5, 6
Billing cycle 0, 1, 12, 13
Session type states
Active require_credit true, archived, require_credit false, updated name/color
Client and purchase states
Logged-in purchase, guest purchase, late activation, archived client, pre-P3.1 legacy purchase
Payment states
Successful first payment, failed first paid invoice, overdue renewal, recharge success, refunded paid invoice
Environments
QA or staging web app with Payment and Booking feature flag control
Stripe test mode for paid, failed, overdue, and recharge scenarios
Data access to purchases, invoices, snapshots, issuance markers, and client balances
Tools
Stripe Dashboard or event simulator for invoice lifecycle and recharge events
API client or Postman for package, purchase, and session type validation
Application logs and event traces for activation and issuance sequencing
Database or admin inspection for snapshot persistence and idempotency checks
Functional Exit Criteria
Critical issuance paths validated across one-time, recurring, trial, recharge, and late activation
No unresolved P0 defects in balance integrity, duplicate issuance, or missed-cycle logic
Snapshot and feature-flag behavior confirmed against agreed product rules
Non-functional Exit Criteria
Webhook or retry behavior does not create duplicate issuance
Late-activation flow updates balance within acceptable delay for the chosen implementation model
Documentation Exit Criteria
Open conflicts and pending BE decisions are recorded before detailed TC generation
High-level strategy analysis is accepted as upstream input for qa-master-workflow

Follow-on inputs for qa-master-workflow, context building, and scenario planning
